raw_consumer_statement: OK. I charged {$41.00} on my Citibank credit card online for two movie tickets from XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>. I later noticed that two additional charges of {$45.00} were fraudulently made on my account on the same day. I disputed the charges that week with Citibank. They gave me a conditional credit of {$91.00} pending their investigation. The next month they reversed the conditional credits after contacting the merchant. When they reversed the charges, I immediately engaged and informed them that those charges were not made by me. They include two charges for XXXX tickets each and some misc Concession charges. Citibank informed me to send an email to their fraud department and inform them of the conditions of the charges which I did. I heard nothing back from their fraud department after sending my letter. Yesterday, my most recent statement arrived with the two fraudulent charges still listed. I called again today and was informed that my XXXX  day window for responding to their letter had expired. I never received a letter from the fraud dept except for the first notice where they had reversed the charges. The fact remains that I disputed the original charges within XXXX  days. They also informed me that the {$50.00} liability per the FCBA for fraudulent charges did not apply because I had used the same card for purchases with them on the same day. I only disputed {$91.00}. Citibank 's response was that there was nothing they could do because the dispute period had expired and that I was responsible for all charges even the {$50.00} limit that I indicated. Citibank is a national company and disputed charges before without issue. Please help me resolve this fraud case with Citbank.
